card.intuition: The practice of writing and managing documentation using the same tools as software development: plain text (Markdown), Version Control (Git), and automated CI/CD pipelines.
card.analogy: Treating your README like your code: instead of a messy Word document living on someone's desktop, the documentation lives in the `docs/` folder of the repo. Every time someone changes a feature, they also change the documentation in the same Pull Request.
when_to_use: Keeping technical documentation in sync with the actual code
Allowing developers to write docs without leaving their IDE
Automatically publishing documentation sites (like Swagger or Docusaurus) on every push
gotchas: Out of date: just because it's 'Code' doesn't mean it updates itself. You still need to enforce 'Docs must be updated' in code reviews.
Complexity: for non-technical users (like Marketing), editing Markdown in a Git repo might be too difficult compared to a Wiki.

exercise.prompt: Identify the most popular lightweight markup language used for 'Documentation as Code'.
